hero.title: Research
hero.intro: Explore the scientific publications, field studies, and pilot program findings that underpin Green Desert's approach to sustainable biotechnology in Saudi Arabia.

sections.title: Cultivation & Soil Science
sections.subtitle: Field studies, soil analysis, and irrigation research from Saudi pilot sites.

cards.title: Hemp Cultivation in Arid Climates: A Saudi Arabian Field Trial
cards.excerpt: An 18-month field study assessing industrial hemp growth under high-temperature, low-rainfall conditions across three pilot sites in the Kingdom.

cards.title: Soil Carbon Sequestration Potential of Cannabis sativa in Desert Soils
cards.excerpt: Laboratory and field analysis of carbon capture rates when hemp is introduced into sandy, nutrient-poor desert soils.

cards.title: Optimising Irrigation Efficiency for Hemp in the Najd Region
cards.excerpt: Comparative analysis of drip, subsurface, and treated wastewater irrigation systems applied to industrial hemp crops.

cards.title: Mycorrhizal Inoculation as a Strategy for Enhancing Hemp Establishment
cards.excerpt: Preliminary results from controlled greenhouse trials using mycorrhizal fungi to accelerate root development in newly seeded hemp.

cards.title: Phytoremediation Capacity of Industrial Hemp in Contaminated Soils
cards.excerpt: Investigation into hemp's ability to extract heavy metals from industrial-adjacent land as a pre-cultivation remediation step.

sections.title: Biomaterials & Construction
sections.subtitle: Hemp fibre composites, hempcrete performance data, and life cycle assessments.

cards.title: Thermal Performance of Hempcrete Panels in Hot-Dry Climates
cards.excerpt: Comparative thermal resistance data for hempcrete vs. conventional concrete block construction under Saudi Arabian summer conditions.

cards.title: Mechanical Properties of Hemp Fibre Composites for Structural Applications
cards.excerpt: Tensile strength, compression, and flexural testing results for hemp-reinforced bio-composites targeted at low-load building elements.

cards.title: Pilot Hempcrete Wall System: Riyadh Residential Build
cards.excerpt: Documentation of the first hempcrete wall panel installation in a Saudi residential context, including thermal monitoring data.

cards.title: CO₂ Absorption in Lime-Hemp Binder Systems over 24 Months
cards.excerpt: Longitudinal study measuring ongoing carbonation and CO₂ sequestration in cured hempcrete samples.

cards.title: Life Cycle Assessment: Hemp vs. Conventional Building Materials
cards.excerpt: Full cradle-to-gate LCA comparing hemp-based panels against cement block, gypsum board, and mineral wool insulation.

sections.title: Circular Economy & Policy
sections.subtitle: Regulatory frameworks, economic feasibility studies, and Vision 2030 alignment.

cards.title: Regulatory Frameworks for Industrial Hemp in GCC Countries
cards.excerpt: Overview and comparison of current legal status, licensing pathways, and THC limits across Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Bahrain.

cards.title: Economic Viability of Industrial Hemp Value Chains in Saudi Arabia
cards.excerpt: Feasibility assessment of a domestic hemp processing industry: cost structure, market sizing, and revenue potential across fibre, hurd, and seed oil.

cards.title: Waste Stream Valorisation: Hemp Hurd as a Raw Material for Particle Board
cards.excerpt: Assessment of hemp hurd's suitability as a substitute feedstock for low-emission particle board manufacturing.

cards.title: Aligning Industrial Hemp with Saudi Vision 2030 Environmental Goals
cards.excerpt: Mapping hemp cultivation and processing value chains to Vision 2030 sustainability KPIs and National Environment Strategy targets.

cards.title: Water Footprint Analysis of Hemp versus Conventional Crops in Arid Zones
cards.excerpt: Comparative water consumption data across full crop cycles for hemp, alfalfa, wheat, and date palm under Saudi Arabian conditions.
